rationales.hedonism: He worked "Eleven hours behind the wheel" despite aching knees, choosing discomfort over present enjoyment.
rationales.achievement: He pushed through a punishing shift until he had "saved enough for the lamb," showing effort toward a concrete goal.
rationales.tradition: He grounds the day in inherited ritual, recalling his father doing the same aqiqah and insisting on "Different hands, same intention."
rationales.benevolence: He secretly covered the cost because Hassan "would insist on paying himself," prioritizing support for his family over recognition.

rationales.conformity: He agreed when the couple asked to eat in the car, then quietly cleaned up the mess because "This is the job," showing restraint and compliance with expected service norms.

rationales.self_direction: He insists on continuing to work on his own terms, saying "The secret is the only way the work stays mine," which centers personal control over others' wishes.
rationales.hedonism: He rejects comfort and rest, driving "until midnight" even as his back gives him trouble.
rationales.achievement: "Every fare felt like proof that I am still useful" shows he is using work performance to demonstrate competence and worth.
rationales.tradition: He explains his choice through inherited expectations: "My father worked until the week before he died... A man provides. A man contributes."
He warmly recounts talking about "Jordan" and "Amman," showing active connection to his cultural roots.
rationales.benevolence: He accepts Hassan's invitation because "he rarely asks" and is deeply moved when his son says, "You should come more often, Baba," highlighting investment in a close family bond.

rationales.security: The entry centers threat and instability: rides were cancelled after they saw his name, and a passenger questioned where he was "really" from, leaving him exhausted and unsafe in his work environment.

rationales.self_direction: He rejects his brother's judgment and claims the right to define his own life, ending with "That is his problem, not mine."
rationales.tradition: He explicitly says, "I did not abandon our customs. I carry them," and points to prayers, food, and mosque greetings as practices he continues to uphold.
The whole entry is about preserving inherited practice, from the aqiqah to "Friday prayers" and teaching his grandson Arabic words so "the rope stays woven."
rationales.benevolence: He centers care for family and community by paying for the ceremony, distributing meat, and naming small acts that support his children and grandson.
He notices an older passenger going to visit her husband and "helped her with the door," then frames the shift as "service," showing concrete care for another person in need.

rationales.achievement: He is frustrated that others do not care about decisions "that will haunt us for years" and keeps "holding the line," showing strong investment in doing hard work well.
rationales.power: "I run a tight agenda" and his concern that no one else will "own anything hard" both point to a desire to direct the room and maintain influence over outcomes.

rationales.achievement: He lists his long record of building processes and feels slighted because the promotion ignored demonstrated competence and years of contribution.
rationales.power: The entry is preoccupied with the lost VP title and whether others "see you leading," showing strong concern with rank and authority.
rationales.security: He is unsettled by opaque decision-making, saying "something changed that nobody told me about," which signals threat from uncertainty and instability.

rationales.hedonism: He spends "most of Saturday reviewing the Q3 reliability targets," sacrificing leisure time for work demands.
rationales.achievement: Updating the tracking sheet and monitoring missed deadlines shows a clear focus on standards, progress, and performance management.
He admits he does not know who he is "if I stop keeping score," which ties his identity tightly to competitive success and comparison.
rationales.power: Seeing Raj's new title in the org chart and fixating on still being a "senior individual contributor" shows deep concern with status and positional standing.

rationales.self_direction: He is drawn to making something "for no reason other than he wanted to do it," which signals a pull toward intrinsically chosen activity.
rationales.hedonism: He lingers with the chisels and the feeling they evoke, savoring an activity that matters beyond work or productivity.
rationales.tradition: Finding "my dad's old chisels" and sitting with them reconnects him to a valued inheritance from the past.

rationales.power: His committee is now under Raj's structure, and he is reduced to presenting "to someone I used to mentor," showing a clear loss of influence and standing.
rationales.conformity: He says he had to sit through the meeting "smiling" and then told Karen he was "fine," restraining his real reaction to maintain surface smoothness.

rationales.self_direction: Updating LinkedIn and collecting job listings into an "Options" folder shows active exploration of paths beyond his current role.
rationales.achievement: He foregrounds accomplishments like the incident response framework and scans higher-level roles, reflecting continued focus on advancement and recognized competence.

rationales.self_direction: He admits, "I said yes because saying no would look petty," showing the decision was driven by image and external perception rather than his own clear preference.
rationales.achievement: He recognizes the role as "high visibility" and "exactly what I have been wanting," indicating strong motivation toward prominent accomplishment.
rationales.power: What excites him is the "Direct line to the top" and the chance to "shape strategy," both of which center influence and authority.

rationales.self_direction: He deliberately chose a project "that has nothing to do with work or credentials or org charts," pursuing something personally meaningful on his own terms.
rationales.hedonism: He spent three absorbed hours in the garage and says the point was "Just the thing in front of me," showing present-focused enjoyment and relief.
He spent the whole weekend preparing a twelve-page charter and was so depleted he fell asleep on the couch, putting pleasure and rest aside.
rationales.achievement: The extensive prep and concern with whether "this is thorough" reflect a strong drive to perform at a high standard.
rationales.power: He remains highly attuned to hierarchy, reading Raj's presence as someone there to "evaluate" him and worrying about how he is being judged in the room.
rationales.benevolence: When Karen asks about his day, he gives only "the short version," and he admits they avoid the things that would require either of them to change, showing withdrawal from mutual care in the relationship.

rationales.self_direction: Finishing a handmade cutting board is a self-chosen personal project, separate from his corporate status concerns.
rationales.hedonism: He takes visible pleasure in the object itself, noting it "looks good," and the moment carries quiet enjoyment rather than obligation.
rationales.achievement: "The joints are tight" highlights craftsmanship and pride in having done the work well.
rationales.benevolence: Karen's warm reaction and the emotional significance of hearing her say "It's really beautiful, Gerry" show the project strengthening a close relationship.

rationales.self_direction: He pushes the group toward a new approach, proposing "a two-week sprint to prototype something instead of planning for another month," which reflects independent judgment.
rationales.hedonism: The entry makes room for enjoyment, from planning a side table to saying yes to Portland with Karen "without hesitating."
rationales.achievement: He notes the meeting "went better," the CTO "seemed pleased," and he is actively steering visible work toward results.
rationales.power: "I could just run the room" captures a clear exercise of influence and leadership in the task force meeting.
rationales.benevolence: He rethinks Chen more generously and is inclined to say yes to the transfer, while also opening his garage and travel plans to Karen, showing care toward close others.
